rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Appearence At Deposition, And For Deposition Testimoney; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Selene Ballonoff; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Daniel A. Conrad In Support
rulings.Rulings: Thomas A. Lucas's Motion for Order Compelling Appearance at Deposition, and for Deposition Testimony and for Award of Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Ballonoff failed to appear and submit to her deposition on four separate occasions, including one ordered by this Court on April 8, 2015. Ballonoff must appear for her deposition no later than July 31, 2015. A failure to appear by that date will result in evidentiary sanctions being imposed against Ms. Ballonoff after a properly noticed motion. Monetary sanctions are awarded to Lucas. He shall prepare a detailed declaration of all costs and attorney's fees incurred and submit it with the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling MUST notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. (7/15/2015). A failure to notify the Court and opposing counsel prior to 4:00 p.m. (7/15/2015) will mean that the losing party accedes to the tentative ruling; and no further argument or hearing would be scheduled.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Admissions Set One, Including Facts, Persons And Documents, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Selene Ballonoff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Admission Set One and Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Lucas objected to or denied all of Ballonoff's RFAs and filed detailed supplemental explanations on July 1, 2015. Ballonoff failed to properly serve the motion. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative order MUST contact the Court and opposing counsel prior to 7/15/2015, at 4:00 p.m. Failing to contact the opposing counsel AND the Court prior to 7/15/2015, at 4:00 p.m. means that the losing party has accepted the tentative ruling; and no further argument would then be scheduled.
